title: Documentation Directory
content:
# Documentation Directory

Index of all workspace documents. Start with this document to navigate workspace knowledge.

## Specs

(none yet)

## References

| ID | Title | Description |
|----|-------|-------------|
| el-4bh | Documentation Directory | This document — index of all docs |
| el-3y1 | Slack App Integration - Architecture Context | Current state of Slack integration, what's done vs remaining gaps, key types and methods |

## How-To Guides

(none yet)

## Explanations

(none yet)

## Decision Logs

(none yet)

## Context
Read the architecture context doc first: `sf document show el-3y1`

The scheduler already references `*SlackAlerter` throughout (see `internal/scheduler/scheduler.go` and `worker_pool.go`), but the actual `SlackAlerter` struct and its methods don't exist yet. The file `internal/scheduler/slack_alerts.go` needs to be created.

## What to do

### Create `internal/scheduler/slack_alerts.go`

```go
package scheduler

import (
    "context"
    "checker/internal/db"
    "checker/internal/models"
    "checker/internal/slack"
    "github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
)

type SlackAlerter struct {
    client         *slack.SlackClient
    repo           db.Repository
    defaultChannel string
}

func NewSlackAlerter(client *slack.SlackClient, repo db.Repository, defaultChannel string) *SlackAlerter {
    if client == nil {
        return nil
    }
    return &SlackAlerter{client: client, repo: repo, defaultChannel: defaultChannel}
}
```

### Implement `SendAlert` method

```go
func (sa *SlackAlerter) SendAlert(ctx context.Context, checkDef models.CheckDefinition, status models.CheckStatus)
```

Logic:
1. Check if silenced: `sa.repo.IsCheckSilenced(ctx, checkDef.UUID, checkDef.Project)` — if silenced, log and return
2. Build `slack.CheckAlertInfo` from checkDef fields:
   - UUID=checkDef.UUID, Name=checkDef.Name, Project=checkDef.Project
   - Group=checkDef.GroupName, CheckType=checkDef.Type
   - Message=status.Message, IsHealthy=false, Frequency=checkDef.Duration
3. Check for existing unresolved thread: `sa.repo.GetUnresolvedThread(ctx, checkDef.UUID)`
   - If found (thread.ThreadTs != ""): post thread reply via `sa.client.SendAlertReply(ctx, thread.ChannelID, thread.ThreadTs, alertInfo)` and return
4. If no existing thread (new failure): post new alert via `sa.client.PostAlert(ctx, sa.defaultChannel, alertInfo)`
5. Track the new thread: `sa.repo.CreateSlackThread(ctx, checkDef.UUID, sa.defaultChannel, messageTs, messageTs)`

### Implement `HandleRecovery` method

```go
func (sa *SlackAlerter) HandleRecovery(ctx context.Context, checkDef models.CheckDefinition)
```

Logic:
1. Get unresolved thread: `sa.repo.GetUnresolvedThread(ctx, checkDef.UUID)` — if none found, return silently
2. Build CheckAlertInfo with IsHealthy=true, Message="Check is healthy again"
3. Post resolution + update original: `sa.client.SendResolve(ctx, alertInfo, thread.ThreadTs, thread.ChannelID)`
4. Mark resolved: `sa.repo.ResolveThread(ctx, checkDef.UUID)`
5. Clear slack columns: `sa.repo.UpdateSlackThread(ctx, checkDef.UUID, "", "")`

All errors should be logged but NOT returned — alert failures must never block check execution.

### Verify

- `go build ./...` must pass
- `go vet ./...` must pass

## Reference code
The old `scheduler/slack_alerts.go` (top-level, uses `my/checker` imports) has the same logic but targeting old types. Use it as reference but write for the new architecture types.

## Key files to read
- `internal/scheduler/scheduler.go` — see how executeCheck calls SendAlert and HandleRecovery
- `internal/slack/client.go` — available methods on SlackClient
- `internal/slack/models.go` — CheckAlertInfo struct
- `internal/models/slack.go` — SlackAlertThread, AlertSilence
- `scheduler/slack_alerts.go` — old implementation for logic reference (DO NOT modify)

## Documentation
- `sf document search "slack"` before starting

## Context
Read the architecture context doc first: `sf document show el-3y1`

Three files from the old architecture use `my/checker` imports (which doesn't match the current module name `checker`). They are dead code — their functionality has been reimplemented in the `internal/` packages.

## What to do

### Delete these files entirely:

1. **`slack/interactions.go`** — Old slash-command handler using `my/checker/config` and `my/checker/status` imports. In-memory silence store. Replaced by `internal/web/slack_handlers.go` which uses DB-backed silences and proper imports.

2. **`scheduler/slack_alerts.go`** — Old Slack alert functions using `my/checker/config`, `my/checker/internal/slack`, `my/checker/projects` imports. Uses undefined global variables. Replaced by the new `internal/scheduler/slack_alerts.go` (SlackAlerter struct with dependency injection).

3. **`alerts/slack_app.go`** — Old alerter stub using `my/checker/config` and `my/checker/internal/slack` imports. Creates a new SlackClient on every message (inefficient). No longer needed — the new SlackAlerter in the scheduler handles this.

### Also check and clean up:

- If `slack/` directory becomes empty after deleting `interactions.go`, remove the directory
- If `scheduler/` (top-level, NOT `internal/scheduler/`) directory becomes empty, remove it
- If `alerts/` directory has other files, leave them. If only `slack_app.go` remains, check if other files reference the package

### Verify

- `go build ./...` must pass (these files DON'T compile anyway due to wrong imports)
- `go vet ./...` must pass
- `go test ./...` must pass

## Documentation
- `sf document search "slack"` before starting
- No documentation updates needed for deletion

## Context
Multiple worker branches from previous attempts target the OLD architecture (cmd/main_checker.go, config/functions.go, etc.) and cannot be merged into the current master which uses internal/ packages. They must be cleaned up.

## What to do

Delete these LOCAL branches (they are stale and superseded):

```bash
git branch -D agent/e-worker-1/el-31r-repository-methods-for-slack-t
git branch -D agent/e-worker-1/el-33h-slack-slash-command-endpoint-f
git branch -D agent/e-worker-1/el-3rd-slack-web-api-client-package
git branch -D agent/e-worker-1/el-55g-slack-interactive-endpoint-sil
git branch -D agent/e-worker-1/el-5yj-config-and-documentation-for-s
git branch -D agent/e-worker-1/el-m3g-integrate-slack-alerts-into-sc
git branch -D agent/e-worker-1/el-u8p-slack-bot-client-block-kit-mes
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-1d4-db-migration-slackalertthreads
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-263-db-schema-alertsilences-table
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-31r-repository-methods-for-slack-t
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-3xr-github-actions-ci-workflow-lin
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-40r-create-multi-stage-dockerfile
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-40rp-verify-linting-and-all-tests-p
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-4yy-slack-app-configuration-and-st
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-5fk-fix-build-update-golangorgxnet
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-66t-integrate-slack-client-into-sc
git branch -D agent/e-worker-2/el-7x1-railway-config-env-var-support
git branch -D d-steward-1/docs/auto-updates
```

Also delete any remote tracking branches if they exist:
```bash
git push origin --delete <branch-name>
```
for any branches that have remotes (check with `git branch -r`).

DO NOT delete `master` or the current working branch.

## Verify
- `git branch` shows only `master` and any active working branches
- No stale remote branches remain
Task assigned: DB migration + PostgreSQL repository methods for Slack [Priority: 1]
Task assigned: Delete stale worker branches [Priority: 3]

Review of el-38b (Delete stale worker branches): The worker successfully deleted 17 of 18 stale local branches and all stale remote branches. No code commits were produced (branch cleanup task), so I marked the merge status as not_applicable.

One branch could NOT be deleted: agent/e-worker-2/el-40rp-verify-linting-and-all-tests-p — it is currently checked out in another worktree (shown with + in git branch output). It will need to be deleted after that worktree is cleaned up. Please create a follow-up task or handle it during worktree cleanup if appropriate.
Task assigned: Create SlackAlerter struct in internal/scheduler [Priority: 1]
